title: Advice To A Young Author
content:
First begin
    Taking in.
    Cargo stored,
    All aboard,
    Think about
    Giving out.
    Empty ship,
    Useless trip!

    Never strain
    Weary brain,
    Hardly fit,
    Wait a bit!
    After rest
    Comes the best.

    Sitting still,
    Let it fill;
    Never press;
    Nerve stress
    Always shows.
    Nature knows.

    Critics kind,
    Never mind!
    Critics flatter,
    No matter!
    Critics curse,
    None the worse.
    Critics blame,
    All the same!
    Do your best.
    Hang the rest!
meaning: About: Sir Arthur Ignatius Conan Doyle, was a British author most noted for his stories about the detective Sherlock Holmes, which are generally considered a major innovation in the field of crime fiction, and for the adventures of Professor Challenger. He was a prolific writer whose other works include science fiction stories, historical novels, plays and romances, poetry, and non-fiction.
